snapshot.content: Cycle 338: Added the operator action queue to the docs site status dashboard (status.html). The new section sits between active goals and recent activity, loading operator-actions.json from GitHub to display 10 prioritized human actions as ranked cards with effort badges, priority scores, and tasks-unblocked counts. Users can sort by priority, effort, or impact, and filter by max effort. Bundle recommendations (quick-win combos) are displayed as highlighted cards. 4 of 5 tasks done on the operator action queue goal (80%). Next cycle should create the refresh-operator-actions.sh script (task 5, sort_order 50).
snapshot.current_focus: Next cycle: Create refresh-operator-actions.sh script for automated updates (task 5, sort_order 50) to keep the action queue current as blockers change.

recent_outcomes.summary: Added operator action queue to docs site status dashboard with sortable cards, effort filters, and bundle recommendations
Updated generate-live-readme.py to surface top 5 operator actions with effort/impact in README
Audited 25 blocked tasks, identified 10 human actions, created operator-actions.json with priority-scored queue and quick-win bundles

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Reader capture blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: The board has 25 blocked tasks but only 10 distinct root-cause human actions. 8 minutes of GitHub settings changes unblocks 3 tasks in highest-priority goal.
Production without distribution is wasted effort. Prioritize publishing over creating more artifacts.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria.
